eval_results.name: layer-hierarchy-definition
eval_results.key_finding: All three layers L0/L1/L2 correctly defined with hierarchy relationship and concrete examples

eval_results.name: naming-convention
eval_results.key_finding: ARCHITECTURE.md for L0 and *.arch.md for L1/L2 correctly specified with case convention explanation

eval_results.name: doc-location-rules
eval_results.key_finding: Correct doc paths generated for all 3 modules with colocation principle explained

eval_results.name: cross-skill-compatibility
eval_results.key_finding: Integration with arch-search and arch-update explained; shared config points identified

strengths: Precise and consistent L0/L1/L2 layer definitions with clear scope boundaries
Correct colocation placement rules applied to multi-module projects
Clear articulation of how shared config ensures cross-skill consistency
weaknesses: As a configuration skill, it provides definitions but does not execute actions directly
Some edge cases (monorepo with nested modules) may need additional placement guidance
recommendations: Add explicit guidance for monorepo and nested module structures
Provide a validation command to check if existing docs follow arch-common conventions
Document upgrade path when arch-common conventions change between versions
